result:
I would make the truth matter again.

Not “truth” as dogma or consensus, but the kind that can be checked, tested, lived. I’d weave it into the air like a new physical law: the more power you have, the harder it becomes to lie without everybody—yourself included—hearing the clang of the lie. A reflex, not a choice. Presidents, influencers, CEOs, parents, lovers: all of us suddenly acoustically naked when we bend facts to serve our fears or our wallets. No more hidden data sets, no more curated outrage, no more bedtime stories that whole societies must pretend to believe so a few can sleep peacefully while others grieve awake.

With that single edit to the operating code of the species, everything else—climate cooperation, fair markets, medicine that heals instead of advertises—would stop being fairy-tale wishes and start being engineering problems we could actually solve together.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
